title: salix arctica var. antiplasta
intro: Salix arctica var. antiplasta, also known as Arctic willow or dwarf Arctic willow, is a subspecies of the Arctic willow plant, a small shrub native to the Arctic regions of North America and Europe. The bark of Salix arctica var. antiplasta has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the bark of Salix arctica var. antiplasta can be made into a tea or infusion, or dried and used as a supplement to harness its medicinal properties.
